title: Systems and methods for use in approving transactions, based on biometric data
abstract: Systems and methods are provided for use in processing transactions based on biometric data used in connection with authenticating consumers to payment accounts to which the transactions are directed. One exemplary method includes causing a biometric associated with a consumer to be received, when the consumer presents a payment device for funding a transaction with a merchant via a payment account associated with the payment device. The method also includes appending, by a computing device, a biometric score to an authorization request associated with the transaction at the merchant where the biometric score is indicative of a degree of match between the received biometric and a reference biometric, and transmitting, by the computing device, the authorization request whereby an issuer associated with the payment account is able to utilize the biometric score in determining to approve or decline the transaction.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A computer-implemented method for use in processing transactions, based on biometric data used in connection with authenticating consumers to payment accounts to which the transactions are directed, the method comprising: receiving a biometric associated with a consumer, when the consumer presents a payment device for funding a transaction with a merchant via a payment account associated with the payment device; appending, by a computing device, a biometric score to an authorization request associated with the transaction at the merchant, the biometric score indicative of a degree of match between the received biometric and a reference biometric stored at the payment device; and transmitting, by the computing device, the authorization request, wherein the biometric score is adapted for an issuer associated with the payment account to utilize the biometric score in determining to approve or decline the transaction. 2. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 , wherein appending the biometric score to the authorization request includes appending the biometric score to a data element of the authorization request, the authorization request being consistent with an ISO 8583 standard; and wherein transmitting the authorization request includes transmitting the authorization request to a payment network. 3. The computer-implemented method of claim 2, wherein the data element includes one of data elements 48 and 55. 4. The computer-implemented method of claim 2, further comprising authenticating, by the computing device, the consumer based on the biometric score; and wherein transmitting the authorization request includes transmitting the authorization request only when the consumer is authenticated. 5. The computer-implemented method of claim 4, further comprising declining, by the computing device, the transactions when the consumer is not authenticated. 6. The computer-implemented method of claim 4, further comprising appending to the authorization request, by the computing device, one or more of a result of the authentication and an indication of the biometric received from the consumer. 7. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, wherein the biometric is associated with one of a fingerprint, a retina, and a voice. 8. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, wherein the computing device includes a point-of-sale terminal; and wherein receiving the biometric associated with the consumer includes prompting, by the POS terminal, the consumer to provide the biometric to a reader for capture. 9. The computer-implemented method of claim 8, wherein the reader is included with the payment device; and wherein receiving the biometric associated with the consumer includes causing the biometric to be captured by the reader included with the payment device. 10. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, further comprising receiving an authorization reply, from an issuer, either approving or declining the transaction. 11. A computer-implemented method for use in approving transactions, based on biometric data used to authenticate consumers to payment accounts in connection with the transactions, the method comprising: receiving an authorization request for a transaction by a consumer to a payment account, the authorization request including a biometric score indicating a degree of match between a biometric received for the consumer in connection with the transaction and a reference biometric; 

 determining, by a computing device, based at least in part on the biometric score, to approve or decline the transaction associated with authorization request, whereby the biometric score provides an additional indicator regarding fraudulent and/or suspicions conditions of the transaction. 12. The computer-implemented method of claim 11, wherein determining to approve or decline the transaction includes determining to approve or decline the transaction based at least in part on a fraud score for the transaction. 13. The computer-implemented method of claim 12, wherein the authorization request includes the fraud score. 14. The computer-implemented method of claim 13, further comprising generating the fraud score based, at least in part, on the biometric score. 15. The computer-implemented method of claim 11, further comprising comparing, by the computing device, the biometric score to at least one threshold; and wherein determining to approve or decline the transaction includes approving the transaction when the biometric score satisfies the at least one threshold and declining the transaction when the biometric score fails to satisfy the at least one threshold. 16. The computer-implemented method of claim 15, further comprising comparing, by the computing device, a fraud score for the transaction to at least one fraud score threshold; and wherein determining to approve or decline the transaction further includes approving the transaction when the fraud score satisfies the at least one fraud score threshold and declining the transaction when the fraud score fails to satisfy the at least one fraud score threshold. 17. The computer-implemented method of claim 11, further comprising generating an authorization reply, in response to the authorization request, either approving or declining the transaction based on said determination. 18. A non-transitory computer readable storage media including computer executable instructions for use in processing transactions, which when executed by at least one processor, cause the at least one processor to: cause a biometric associated with a consumer to be received, when the consumer presents a payment device for funding a transaction via a payment account associated with the payment device; authenticate the consumer, in connection with the transaction, when a biometric score satisfies at least one threshold, the biometric score indicative of a degree of match between the received biometric and a reference biometric stored at the payment device; generate an authorization request for the transaction when the consumer is authenticated, and append the biometric score to the authorization request; and transmit the authorization request to an issuer associated with the payment account, wherein the issuer is able to utilize the biometric score in determining whether to approve or decline the transaction. 19. The non-transitory computer-readable storage medium of claim 18, wherein the computer executable instructions, when executed by the at least one processor, further cause the at least one processor to append to the authorization request one or more of a result of the authentication and an indication of a type of the biometric received from the consumer. 20. A system comprising: the non-transitory computer readable storage media of claim 18; and a fraud engine configured to; receive the authorization request, as the authorization request is transmitted by the at least one processor to the issuer; and append a fraud score for the transaction to the authorization request.
